subtasks.testStrategy: Review report with stakeholders to verify completeness and accuracy of comparisons.

subtasks.title: Conduct final review of all hardened agent definitions
subtasks.description: Perform a comprehensive review of all 22 hardened agent definitions to ensure consistency and quality.
subtasks.details: Use the quality checklist to review each hardened agent definition. Verify that all required improvements have been implemented and that agents consistently follow the hardening framework. Document any remaining issues and create follow-up tasks.
subtasks.testStrategy: Conduct peer review with at least two reviewers per agent category and document review outcomes.

subtasks.title: Update project documentation with hardening results
subtasks.description: Integrate hardening results into project documentation.
subtasks.details: Update relevant project documentation to reflect the hardening process, framework, templates, quality checklist, and validation results. Ensure documentation is accessible and easy to understand for future contributors.
subtasks.testStrategy: Review updated documentation with stakeholders to ensure it accurately reflects the hardening work.

subtasks.title: Create training materials for using hardened agents
subtasks.description: Develop training materials to help team members effectively use the hardened agents.
subtasks.details: Create training materials including tutorials, examples, and best practices for interacting with the hardened agents. Focus on demonstrating how to get actionable, specific guidance rather than generic advice.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test training materials with a sample group of users and gather feedback on clarity and effectiveness.

subtasks.title: Conduct knowledge transfer session on agent hardening
subtasks.description: Share knowledge about the hardening process and results with the team.
subtasks.details: Organize and conduct a knowledge transfer session to explain the research, analysis, hardening, and validation phases. Demonstrate the hardened agents in action and answer questions from team members.
subtasks.testStrategy: Gather feedback from participants to assess understanding and identify areas for clarification.

subtasks.title: Establish ongoing agent definition review process
subtasks.description: Create a process for regularly reviewing and updating agent definitions.
subtasks.details: Design a process for periodic review of agent definitions, including frequency, participants, and criteria. Integrate the quality checklist into the review process and establish procedures for updating the hardening framework as needed.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test the review process with a pilot review cycle and document outcomes for improvement.

tasks.title: Refactor Engineering Standards to Support Pluggable Technology Stacks
tasks.description: Restructure the engineering standards directory to separate universal standards from technology-specific standards, enabling support for multiple programming languages and technology stacks.
tasks.details: Implement a comprehensive refactoring of the standards directory structure: (1) Keep universal standards in standards/code/ including clean-code.md, error-handling.md, and a refactored language-agnostic typing-standards.md that focuses on strict typing concepts rather than mypy-specific flags. (2) Create new standards/stacks/ directory with technology-specific subdirectories, starting with standards/stacks/python/ containing three files: conventions.md (naming conventions, import patterns, Pydantic/dataclass usage patterns), tooling.md (pytest configuration, mypy strict mode settings, ruff rules, uv package management, coverage thresholds), and frameworks.md (FastAPI patterns, dependency injection strategies, Pydantic model best practices). (3) Migrate content from the existing python-conventions.md into the new stacks/python/conventions.md structure. (4) Update all agent definitions that currently reference Python-specific standards to point to the new stacks/python/ location instead. (5) Modify install.sh to copy the new stacks/ directory structure to ~/.claude/standards/stacks/. (6) Create comprehensive documentation explaining the pluggable stack pattern, including how to add new technology stacks (TypeScript, Go, etc.) by simply creating new subdirectories under stacks/. (7) Establish the convention that project-level CLAUDE.md files declare which technology stack is active for that project. This refactoring enables the platform to support multiple programming languages while maintaining universal engineering principles.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify the new directory structure exists with standards/code/ containing universal standards and standards/stacks/python/ containing the three technology-specific files. Confirm python-conventions.md content has been properly migrated to stacks/python/conventions.md. Test that typing-standards.md has been updated to be language-agnostic. Verify all agent definition files have been updated to reference stacks/python/ instead of direct Python standard references. Confirm install.sh successfully copies the stacks/ directory structure. Test that the documentation clearly explains how to add new technology stacks and that project-level CLAUDE.md can specify active stacks.

tasks.title: Implement .meta/ Reconciliation System with Git Hooks
tasks.description: Create a git hook and agent workflow system that automatically keeps .meta/description.md files synchronized with code changes through a fast post-commit marking system and asynchronous regeneration process.
tasks.details: Implementation consists of three components: (1) Post-commit git hook (hooks/post-commit) that runs git diff --name-only HEAD~1 HEAD to identify changed files, walks up each file's directory tree using dirname to find .meta/description.md files, and marks stale ones by writing to .meta/stale.json with timestamp and list of changed files - this must complete in <100ms. (2) Reconcile command/agent mode that reads .meta/stale.json files, regenerates affected .meta/description.md files by re-reading current directory contents and running AI generation, then removes stale markers - this can be integrated into brownfield-bootstrapper as a --reconcile mode. (3) Optional pre-push hook (hooks/pre-push) that scans for .meta/stale.json files and warns/blocks if any exist. The post-commit hook uses shell scripting for speed: find parent directories with while read -r file; do dir=$(dirname "$file"); while [[ "$dir" != "/" ]]; do if [[ -f "$dir/.meta/description.md" ]]; then echo "$file" >> "$dir/.meta/stale.json"; fi; dir=$(dirname "$dir"); done; done. The reconcile agent reads stale.json, processes each directory's current state, regenerates descriptions, and rm stale.json when complete. This implements the ISS reconciliation loop pattern - keeping derived state (.meta/) in sync with source state (code) through reactive updates.
tasks.testStrategy: Test post-commit hook speed with time command on repos with 100+ files (must be <100ms), verify stale.json creation after commits that modify files in directories with .meta/description.md, test directory tree walking by creating nested structures and confirming all parent .meta/ files are marked stale, test reconcile mode by creating stale markers and verifying regenerated descriptions reflect current directory state, test pre-push hook by creating stale.json files and confirming warning/blocking behavior, verify integration with brownfield-bootstrapper --reconcile flag, and test end-to-end workflow: make code change → commit → verify stale markers → run reconcile → verify updated descriptions and cleared markers.

tasks.title: Harden Tier 1 Agents (Critical Path)
tasks.description: Apply hardening framework from docs/research/agent-hardening-research.md to the 5 critical-path agents: code-reviewer, security-reviewer, backend-developer, verifier, sem. These run on every code change or orchestration step and need concrete heuristics, decision trees, output formats, coordination models, and error recovery.
tasks.details: Each agent must meet all 10 success criteria from the research doc. Use the template from Section 6, gap analysis from Section 5, heuristic catalog from Appendix A, and model selection from Appendix B. Target 80-200 lines per agent.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify each hardened agent has: 2+ example blocks in description, Before Starting section with file paths, numbered process steps, output format template, boundaries with You Do / You Do NOT, error recovery, coordination section, and line count 80-200.

tasks.title: Harden Tier 2 Agents (High Impact)
tasks.description: Harden architect, product-manager, frontend-developer, devops-engineer, domain-modeler using the research framework. These are high-impact agents with significant gaps.
tasks.details: Same hardening criteria as Tier 1. Architect needs ADR template and smell detection. PM needs PRD template and Socratic questions. Frontend dev needs parity with backend dev. DevOps needs Docker/CI checklists. Domain modeler needs grep patterns.
tasks.testStrategy: Same 10-point success criteria as Tier 1.

tasks.title: Harden Tier 3 Agents (Medium Impact)
tasks.description: Harden product-owner, technical-writer, process-evaluator, brownfield-bootstrapper, architect-reviewer. These are medium-impact — some already decent, others need templates and validation processes.
tasks.details: PO needs acceptance criteria template. Tech writer needs doc templates. Process evaluator needs trend methodology. Brownfield bootstrapper needs error recovery. Architect-reviewer needs coordination and grep commands.
tasks.testStrategy: Same 10-point success criteria as Tier 1.

tasks.title: Harden Tier 4 Agents (Lower Priority / Already Good)
tasks.description: Harden ux-designer, ui-designer, code-simplifier, frontend-dashboard-refactorer, multi-tenant-auditor, project-bootstrapper, gemini-analyzer. Most are already good or low-frequency. Focus on adding coordination sections and examples.
tasks.details: UX/UI designers need substantial expansion. Code-simplifier just needs coordination. frontend-dashboard-refactorer and multi-tenant-auditor are model agents — add coordination only. Evaluate if gemini-analyzer is still needed.
tasks.testStrategy: Same 10-point success criteria. UX/UI must reach 80+ lines.
